free_text: Excitation functions and angular distributions for
the reaction C12(He3,n)O14
The Rice University 5.5 MeV Van de
Graaff accelerator.
A detection efficiency correction, depending on the
energy of neutrons, was applied for the neutrons
counted at various He3 bombarding energies and angles
of detection.
BP
Better
experimental uncertainties.
The thin carbon target on a 0.025 cm tungsten backing
was mounted on a 0.035 cm thick aluminium plate.
A cylindrical plastic scintillator 2.2 cm in
diameter and 1.8 cm long was used to detect the
positrons
A 2.54 cmx 2.54 cm NaI(TI) crystal was used for
the detection of 2.31 MeV gamma rays.
The error of this neutron cross
section determination is estimated less than 30%.
Fig. 1, page 269.
B10 enriched BF3 counter (modified long
counter). The counter tube was 2.5 cm in diameter and
15 cm in length and was embedded in a paraffin
cylinder 15 cm in diameter and 12.2 cm long.
The carbon target used for the excitation functions
and for the total yield was made by cracking carbon
from methyl iodide (CH3I) on a tungsten blank of
1.905 cm diameter and 0.025 cm thickness. The
thickness of the carbon target was about 60 keV for 3
MeV He3 particles. For the study of the angular
distributions, a carbon target was made in the same
way as mentioned above on a thick tantalum backing. It
was about 300 keV thick for 3 MeV He3 ions.
Fig. 1, page 269.
